ddls:
CREATE TABLE "school_districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the school district */,
  "district_type" TEXT /* Type of district (e.g., urban, rural) */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the district is located */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* JSON object storing contact information (phone, email, website) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password */,
  "role" TEXT /* User role (e.g., administrator, educator, policymaker) */,
  "email" TEXT /* User's email address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* User's phone number */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the users table */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the schools table */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the statistic */,
  "stat_type" TEXT /* Type of statistic (e.g., enrollment, test scores, graduation rate) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the statistic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_demographics" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the schools table */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade level of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the student */,
  "special_needs" INTEGER /* Indicator for special needs (0 or 1) */,
  "enrollment_date" TEXT /* Date of enrollment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each school district
State where the district is located
JSON object storing contact information (phone, email, website)
Foreign key linking to the users table
